%% solve(Points, Target) -> int
%% Points: list of [x, y, z] lists.
%% Target: [tx, ty, tz] list.
solve(Points, Target) ->
    case check_k0(Points, Target) of
        {ok, _} -> 0;
        {fail, AllPoints0} ->
            generate_and_check(AllPoints0, Target, 1)
    end.

%% check_k0(Points, Target) -> {ok, SetOfPoints} or {fail, ListOfPoints}
check_k0(Points, Target) ->
    TargetKey = [Target],
    PointsKeys = lists:map(fun([X, Y, Z]) -> [X, Y, Z] end, Points), % Ensure list format for consistency if needed, though input is fine
    
    % Check if target is in initial points
    case lists:member(Target, Points) of
        true -> {ok, Points};
        false -> {fail, Points}
    end.

%% generate_and_check(CurrentAllPoints, Target, K) -> int or -1
generate_and_check(CurrentAllPoints, Target, K) ->
    % 1. Generate the next generation points (Gen K)
    NextGenerationPoints = generate_next_generation(CurrentAllPoints),
    
    % 2. Check if target is in the new generation
    case lists:member(Target, NextGenerationPoints) of
        true -> K;
        false ->
            % 3. If not found, update all points and recurse
            NewAllPoints = lists:append(CurrentAllPoints, NextGenerationPoints),
            
            % Check if no new points were generated (prevents infinite loop if target unreachable)
            if length(NextGenerationPoints) == 0 
            then -1

            end
    end.

            false -> [P | Acc]
        end

-module(solver_module).

solve(Points, Target) ->
    % Convert the target list [tx, ty, tz] to a unique key string
    TargetKey = [Target !! 0, Target !! 1, Target !! 2],
    TargetStr = format("~s,~s,~s", [TargetKey !! 0, TargetKey !! 1, TargetKey !! 2]),

    % Initialize the set of unique points found so far (Gen 0 to Gen k-1)
    InitialPoints = Points,
    InitialPointKeys = lists:map(fun([X,Y,Z]) -> format("~s,~s,~s", [X,Y,Z]) end, InitialPoints),
    PointsSet = lists:keymap(fun([X,Y,Z]) -> format("~s,~s,~s", [X,Y,Z]) end, InitialPoints, fun(P) -> P end),

    % Check for k=0
    case maps:is_key(TargetStr, PointsSet) of
        true -> 0;
        false ->
            % Start iterative generation
            iterate_generations(InitialPoints, PointsSet, 1, TargetStr)
    end.

%% Helper to check if two points have the same coordinates
same_coords(P1, P2) ->
    P1 !! 0 == P2 !! 0 and
    P1 !! 1 == P2 !! 1 and
    P1 !! 2 == P2 !! 2.

%% Iteratively generates generations k=1, 2, 3...
iterate_generations(AllPoints, CurrentSet, K, TargetStr) ->
    % 1. Generate new points for generation K
    NewPoints = generate_next_generation(AllPoints),
    
    % 2. Check if the target is among the newly generated points
    TargetFound = lists:any(fun(P) -> 
        format("~s,~s,~s", [P !! 0, P !! 1, P !! 2]) == TargetStr
    end, NewPoints),

    if TargetFound ->
        K;
    else
        % 3. Update sets and continue
        NewPointKeys = lists:map(fun([X,Y,Z]) -> format("~s,~s,~s", [X,Y,Z]) end, NewPoints),
        UpdatedSet = maps:merge(CurrentSet, maps:from_list(NewPointKeys, true)),
        
        % Add new points to the list for the next iteration's pairing pool
        NextAllPoints = AllPoints ++ NewPoints,

        % Safety break (based on constraints, though theoretical bounds might be larger)
        if K > 500 -> -1; % Arbitrary large limit to prevent infinite loop in case of unexpected behavior
        true -> 
            iterate_generations(NextAllPoints, UpdatedSet, K + 1, TargetStr)
        end
    end.


%% Generates the set of unique points for the next generation K,
%% based on all points generated up to K-1 (provided in AllPoints).
generate_next_generation(AllPoints) ->
    % Use a list of [Point, Key] pairs to manage uniqueness during generation
    {NewPointsList, _} = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, P1) -> 
        lists:foldl(fun(Acc2, P2) ->
            % Pair P1 with P2, ensuring P1 and P2 are distinct points (different coordinates)
            if same_coords(P1, P2) ->
                Acc2
            else
                % Calculate midpoint C
                C = [
                    floor((P1 !! 0 + P2 !! 0) / 2),
                    floor((P1 !! 1 + P2 !! 1) / 2),
                    floor((P1 !! 2 + P2 !! 2) / 2)
                ],
                
                % Check if C has already been generated/added to Acc2
                CKey = format("~s,~s,~s", [C !! 0, C !! 1, C !! 2]),
                
                case maps:is_key(CKey, Acc2) of
                    true -> Acc2;
                    false -> 
                        % Add C and its key to the accumulator map
                        maps:put(CKey, C, Acc2)
                end
            end
        end, Acc, AllPoints)
    end, maps:from_list([], []), AllPoints).
    
    % Extract only the points from the final map
    lists:map(fun(Value) -> Value end, maps:to_list(NewPointsList)).

    point_to_key(_Point) ->

    % 2. InitialKeys: A list of string keys corresponding to initial points for quick k=0 check.

% AllPoints: List of all unique points found up to generation K-1 (Gen 0 to Gen K-1)
% GeneratedPointsKeys: List of keys of points generated in generations 1 to K-1 (used to manage uniqueness)
loop(K, AllPoints, _) ->
    
    % 1. Generate next generation points (Gen K)
    NewPoints = generate_next_generation(AllPoints),
    
    % Check if NewPoints is empty (only one unique point exists in AllPoints, or no pairs possible)

        _ ->

                    NewPointKeys = [point_to_key(P) || P <- NewPoints],
                    
                    % Combine AllPoints and NewPoints to form the new set for next iteration
                    NextAllPoints = lists:union(AllPoints, NewPoints),
                    
                    % Recurse
                    loop(K+1, NextAllPoints, [])
            end
    end.

    let n = length(unique_points);
